SourceText: {F1_SON_5} and his mother are playing dress up during one-on-one time. Let’s see how they have fun together:
text: {F1_SON_5} en sy ma speel aantrek tydens een-tot-een tyd. Kom ons kyk hoe het hulle saam pret:

SourceText: **{F1_MOTHER}**: “I have 5 minutes of one-on-one time to spend with you, {F1_SON_5}. What would you like to do?”
text: **{F1_MOTHER}**: “Ek het 5 minute een-tot-een tyd om saam met jou deur te bring, {F1_SON_5}. Wat wil jy graag doen?”

SourceText: **{F1_SON_5}**: “I want to play dress up!”
text: **{F1_SON_5}**: “Ek wil aantrek speel!”

SourceText: **{F1_MOTHER}**: "Wonderful idea, {F1_SON_5}! I see you are taking out the yellow scarf with blue stripes on it.”
text: **{F1_MOTHER}**: “Wonderlike idee, {F1_SON_5}! Ek sien jy haal die geel serp met die blou strepies uit."

SourceText: **{F1_SON_5}**: "Mommy, it’s for you."
text: **{F1_SON_5}**: “Dis vir Ma."

SourceText: **{F1_MOTHER}**: "Thank you, I love it!"
text: **{F1_MOTHER}**: Dankie, ek's mal daaroor!"

SourceText: **{F1_SON_5}**: "Wear it, Mommy!"
text: **{F1_SON_5}**: "Sit dit aan, Ma!"

SourceText: **{F1_MOTHER}**: “Look how good you are at getting dressed by yourself, {F1_SON_5}!”
text: **{F1_MOTHER}**: “Kyk hoe mooi kan jy jouself al aantrek, {F1_SON_5}!”

SourceText: 🌷We all feel better when we are praised.

SourceText: ✨ How you praise your child is important. Praise should be specific, immediate, and genuine.
text: ✨ Hoe jy jou kind prys, is belangrik. Lof moet spesifiek, onmiddellik en opreg wees.

SourceText: 🌱 You can begin by praising your child for the smallest things, such as playing by themselves quietly for 2 minutes.
text: 🌱 Jy kan begin deur jou kind vir klein goedjies te prys, soos om vir 2 minute stil op hulle eie te speel.

SourceText: Let’s check in on another family to see how the mother praises her son, {F3_SON_4}, during one-on-one time.
text: Kom ons kyk na 'n ander gesin om te sien hoe die ma haar seun, {F3_SON_4}, prys gedurende een-tot-een tyd.

SourceText: **{F3_MOTHER}**: “You found a striped sock and one with dots. Let’s find the socks that match those two.”
text: **{F3_MOTHER}**: “Jy het 'n gestreepte sokkie gekry, en een met kolle. Kom ons soek die sokkies wat by daardie twee pas.”

SourceText: **{F3_MOTHER}**: “Another sock! This one is white. It isn’t easy finding a matching pair. It can be frustrating.”
text: **{F3_MOTHER}**: “Nog 'n sokkie! Hierdie een is wit. Dis nie maklik om 'n paar te kry wat bymekaar pas nie. Dit kan frustrerend wees.”

SourceText: **{F3_MOTHER}**: “You are being very patient and looking very hard, {F3_SON_4}. Well done for trying.”
text: **{F3_MOTHER}**: “Jy is baie geduldig en jy soek baie hard, {F3_SON_4}. Dis mooi dat jy probeer.”

SourceText: **{F3_MOTHER}**: “You found two socks with the same pattern! Well done for looking, {F3_SON_4}!”
text: **{F3_MOTHER}**: “Jy het twee sokkies met dieselfde patroon gekry! Jy het mooi gesoek, {F3_SON_4}!”

SourceText: 🎉 Be enthusiastic and really mean it!

SourceText: 💡 Learn about different emotions. There are 6 basic emotions that each send us a very specific message:
text: 💡 Leer oor verskillende emosies. Daar is 6 basiese emosies wat elk vir ons 'n baie spesifieke boodskap stuur:

SourceText: 😃Happy: Keep doing things that make you feel good.
text: 😃Gelukkig: Hou aan om dinge te doen wat jou goed laat voel.

SourceText: 😔Sad: Something important in your life needs attention.
text: 😔Hartseer: Iets belangriks in jou lewe kort aandag.

SourceText: 🔴Angry: Stand up for ourselves and set limits.
text: 🔴Kwaad: Staan op vir onsself en stel grense.

SourceText: 😣Disgusted: Move away from what we don't like.
text: 😣Weersin: Beweeg weg van dit waarvan ons nie hou nie.

SourceText: 👀Surprised: Stop and pay attention for a moment.
text: 👀Verras: Stop en gee vir 'n oomblik aandag.

SourceText: 🚩Afraid: Run away from danger.
text: 🚩Bang: Hardloop weg van gevaar.

SourceText: This lesson is about how we can stay calm and manage our anger when dealing with stressful moments with our children. 💚
text: Hierdie les gaan oor hoe ons kalm kan bly en ons woede kan beheer wanneer ons stresvolle oomblikke met ons kinders beleef. 💚

SourceText: As you began noticing feelings, you may have noticed feelings of anger, frustration or sadness.
text: Soos jy na gevoelens begin oplet het, sou jy gevoelens van woede, frustrasie of hartseer opgemerk het.

SourceText: 🌷 We love our children, but stress from caregiving and other events can make us feel angry. This is a normal part of life!
text: 🌷 Ons is lief vir ons kinders, maar die stres van versorging en ander gebeure kan ons laat kwaad voel! Dit is 'n normale deel van die lewe!

SourceText: 💡 It is important for us to try to stay calm in order to help manage our anger so we do not hurt others.
text: 💡 Dit is vir ons belangrik om te probeer om kalm te bly en so ons woede te help beheer sodat ons nie ander seermaak nie.

SourceText: When I am angry at my child, I should show that I am angry so they stop misbehaving.
text: Wanneer ek kwaad is vir my kind, moet ek wys dat ek kwaad is, sodat hulle kan ophou met hulle wangedrag.

SourceText: 💛 It can be very difficult when your child is misbehaving, but you should try to manage your own stress and control your anger before doing anything.
text: 💛 Dit kan baie moeilik wees wanneer jou kind hulle wangedra, maar jy moet probeer om jou eie stres en woede te beheer voordat jy enigiets doen.

SourceText: 🌱 Taking deep breaths will help. Being angry may cause you to say or do things you will later regret.
text: 🌱 Om diep asem te haal, sal help. Wanneer jy kwaad is, kan jy dalk dinge sê of doen wat jy later sal berou.

SourceText: 🎉 That’s correct!
text: 🎉 Dis korrek!

SourceText: {F2_GRANDFATHER} is about to spend one-on-one time with {F2_SON_8} but finds the living room in a mess. He notices that this makes him feel angry.
text: {F2_GRANDFATHER} gaan nou een-tot-een tyd saam met {F2_SON_8} deurbring, maar sien die woonkamer is baie deurmekaar. Hy let op dat dit hom kwaad laat voel.

SourceText: **{F2_GRANDFATHER}** (thinking): “I’m feeling really angry. I am tired and my body feels tense. I just want to shout at {F2_SON_8}”.
text: **{F2_GRANDFATHER}** (dink): “Ek voel regtig kwaad. Ek is moeg en my liggaam voel gespanne. Ek wil net op {F2_SON_8} skree”.

SourceText: **{F2_GRANDFATHER}** (thinking): “I will Take a Pause and put on my favourite song.”
text: **{F2_GRANDFATHER}** (dink): “Ek sal 'n bietjie wag en my gunsteling liedjie speel.”

SourceText: **{F2_GRANDFATHER}** (thinking): “I am feeling a little better. I can think more clearly about what to do to support my child.”
text: **{F2_GRANDFATHER}** (dink): “Ek voel 'n klein bietjie beter. Ek kan nou helder dink oor wat om te doen om my kind te ondersteun.”

SourceText: **{F2_GRANDFATHER}**: “{F2_SON_8}, well done for doing your homework. You might feel tired now. Let's take a 5-minute break, and after that, we can play a game together to quickly pick up the toys.”
text: **{F2_GRANDFATHER}**: “{F2_SON_8}, dis mooi dat jy jou huiswerk gedoen het. Jy is dalk nou 'n bietjie moeg. Kom ons breek vir so 5 minute, dan kan ons saam 'n speletjie speel om vinnig die speelgoed op te tel.”

SourceText: **{F2_GRANDFATHER}**: “Thank you for doing your part to keep the house clean, {F2_SON_8}! I’m so proud of you for helping. Oh! It is our one-on-one time. What would you like to do?”
text: **{F2_GRANDFATHER}**: “Dankie dat jy jou kant bring om die huis skoon te hou, {F2_SON_8}! Ek is so trots op jou dat jy help. O! Dis ons een-tot-een tyd. Wat wil jy graag doen?”

SourceText: True or False: When I spend special time with my teen, I need to decide what we will do together.

SourceText: 🚲 You do not have to plan an activity. Your teen can lead! Let them choose as long as it is safe. This will increase their confidence and make it more likely that they enjoy their time with you.

SourceText: Let’s see how {F1_FATHER} and {F1_DAUGHTER_13} spend one-on-one time together:
text: Kom ons kyk hoe {F1_FATHER} en {F1_DAUGHTER_13} een-tot-een tyd saam deurbring:

SourceText: **{F1_FATHER}**: “{F1_DAUGHTER_13}, how was your day?”
text: **{F1_FATHER}**: “{F1_DAUGHTER_13}, hoe was jou dag?”

SourceText: **{F1_DAUGHTER_13}**: “My day was good, {F1_FATHER}. I just came from school.”
text: **{F1_DAUGHTER_13}**: “My dag was lekker, Pa. Ek kom nou van die skool af.”

SourceText: **{F1_FATHER}**: “I have 5 minutes of one-on-one time to spare before I have to drive to work again. What would you like to do together?”
text: **{F1_FATHER}**: “Ek kan 5 minute een-tot-een tyd afknyp voordat ek weer werk toe moet ry. Wat wil jy graag saam met my doen?”

SourceText: **{F1_DAUGHTER_13}**: “I was about to do my Science homework. The topic for the homework is to make something out of recycled materials. Could you please help me with it?”
text: **{F1_DAUGHTER_13}**: “Ek wou nou net begin met my Wetenskap-huiswerk. Die onderwerp van die huiswerk is om iets uit herwinbare materiale te maak. Kan Pa my asseblief daarmee help?”

SourceText: **{F1_FATHER}**: “I would be happy to!”
text: **{F1_FATHER}**: “Ek sal dit met plesier doen!”

SourceText: **{F1_DAUGHTER_13}**: “I thought of building a recycled house using old bottles, boxes, and other recycled materials. Look! I picked these up on my way home. They were thrown away by the side of the road and outside the spaza shops.”
text: **{F1_DAUGHTER_13}**: “Ek het gedink ek sal 'n herwinbare huis bou met ou bottels, bokse en ander herwinbare materiale. Kyk! Ek het hierdie goed op pad huis toe opgetel. Dis langs die pad en buite die spazawinkels weggegooi.”

SourceText: **{F1_FATHER}**: “What a good idea, {F1_DAUGHTER_13}! Sounds like you’ve thought about this a lot and want to put a lot of effort into your homework. How do you want me to help you?”
text: **{F1_FATHER}**: "Wat 'n goeie idee, {F1_DAUGHTER_13}! Dit klink asof jy baie hieroor gedink het en baie moeite met jou huiswerk wil doen. Hoe wil jy hê moet ek jou help?"

SourceText: **{F1_DAUGHTER_13}**: “Could you help me clean these bottles? They will be the pillars of the house. I will make the boxes into the walls and roof of the house. Later, I thought we could paint the boxes and bottles so they look beautiful.”
text: **{F1_DAUGHTER_13}**: “Kan Pa my help om hierdie bottels skoon te maak? Dit gaan die pilare van die huis wees. Ek gaan die bokse gebruik vir die mure en die dak van die huis. Ek het gedink ons kan later die bokse en bottels verf sodat hulle mooi lyk.”

SourceText: **{F1_FATHER}**: “I may have some leftover paint outside the house. I can take a look later.”
text: **{F1_FATHER}**: “Ek het dalk nog 'n bietjie oorskietverf buite. Ek kan later gaan kyk.”

SourceText: (Later in the day)
text: (Later die dag)

SourceText: **{F1_FATHER}**: “Wow, it looks different now! It does not look like you used rubbish at all anymore. You also painted the walls and the roof beautifully! I think your teacher will be proud of you when she sees your homework!”
text: **{F1_FATHER}**: “Sjoe, dit lyk nou anders! Dit lyk glad nie meer asof jy rommel gebruik het nie. Jy het ook die mure en die dak pragtig geverf! Ek dink jou onderwyser gaan baie trots wees wanneer sy jou huiswerk sien!”

SourceText: **{F1_DAUGHTER_13}**: “Thank you for your help, {F1_FATHER}. You know, there were a lot of fun things that happened in school today.”
text: **{F1_DAUGHTER_13}**: “Dankie vir Pa se hulp. Weet Pa, daar het vandag 'n klomp prettige goed by die skool gebeur.”

SourceText: **{F1_FATHER}**: “Go ahead, tell me!”
text: **{F1_FATHER}**: “Maar vertel vir my!”

SourceText: This lesson is about creating routines for spending one-on-one time with your teen. 🔄

SourceText: **{F2_GRANDFATHER}**: “{F2_SON_15}, I really enjoyed spending one-on-one time with you yesterday. I never knew you were so good at soccer!”
text: **{F2_GRANDFATHER}**: “{F2_SON_15}, Ek het dit regtig gister geniet om een-tot-een tyd saam met jou deur te bring. Ek het nooit geweet jy is so goed in sokker nie!”

SourceText: **{F2_SON_15}**: “Aw…thanks, {F2_GRANDFATHER}!”
text: **{F2_SON_15}**: “Ag... dankie, {F2_GRANDFATHER}!”

SourceText: **{F2_GRANDFATHER}**: “It would be great if we could have time together more often. Should we try to do it every day?”
text: **{F2_GRANDFATHER}**: “Dit sou wonderlik wees as ons meer tyd saam kon hê. Sal ons probeer om dit elke dag te doen?”

SourceText: **{F2_SON_15}**: “I guess so…though sometimes I am busy.”
text: **{F2_SON_15}**: “Seker ... alhoewel ek soms besig is.”

SourceText: **{F2_GRANDFATHER}**: “Yeah, that’s true. Let’s make it something that we can really do. I am sure we could find 15 minutes together!”
text: **{F2_GRANDFATHER}**: “Ja, dis waar. Kom ons reël iets wat ons regtig kan doen. Ek is seker ons kan 15 minute saam afknyp!”

SourceText: **{F2_SON_15}**: “Sure, we can try.”
text: **{F2_SON_15}**: “Reg, ons kan probeer.”

SourceText: **{F2_GRANDFATHER}**: “When would be the best time for you?”
text: **{F2_GRANDFATHER}**: “Wanneer sal die beste tyd vir jou wees?”

SourceText: **{F2_SON_15}**: “How about after dinner?”
text: **{F2_SON_15}**: “Wat van na aandete?”

SourceText: **{F2_GRANDFATHER}**: “That could work as long as we clean up first. Let’s start today. And remember, you can choose what we do!”
text: **{F2_GRANDFATHER}**: “Dit kan werk, solank ons eers opruim. Kom ons begin vandag. En onthou, jy kan kies wat ons doen!”

SourceText: **{F2_SON_15}**: “Okay, {F2_GRANDFATHER}. I look forward to it.”
text: **{F2_SON_15}**: “Oukei, {F2_GRANDFATHER}. Ek sien uit daarna.”

SourceText: **{F2_GRANDFATHER}**: “Thanks, {F2_SON_15}. I really appreciate having this time with you. It means a lot to me.”
text: **{F2_GRANDFATHER}**: “Dankie, {F2_SON_15}. Ek waardeer regtig hierdie tyd saam met jou. Dit beteken baie vir my.”

SourceText: Here is a comic about how you can talk about feelings with your teen during one-on-one time.
text: Hier is 'n strokie oor hoe jy tydens een-tot-een tyd met jou tiener oor gevoelens kan praat.

SourceText: {F1_FATHER} and {F1_DAUGHTER_16} are spending one-on-one time by taking a walk together. {F1_DAUGHTER_16} looks very upset.
text: {F1_FATHER} en {F1_DAUGHTER_16} stap saam tydens hulle een-tot-een tyd. {F1_DAUGHTER_16} lyk baie ontsteld.

SourceText: **{F1_FATHER}**: “{F1_DAUGHTER_16}, I noticed you have less energy today and you look sad. Do you want to talk about it?”
text: **{F1_FATHER}**: “{F1_DAUGHTER_16}, ek sien jy het vandag minder energie en jy lyk hartseer. Wil jy daaroor praat?”

SourceText: **{F1_DAUGHTER_16}**: “Oh, {F1_FATHER}. I studied so hard for my maths test and failed. I feel so sad and frustrated.”
text: **{F1_DAUGHTER_16}**: “Ag, Pa, ek het so hard vir my Wiskundetoets geleer en gedruip. Ek voel so hartseer en gefrustreerd.”

SourceText: **{F1_FATHER}**: “That is frustrating, my girl. I know how hard you studied. Maybe we can come up with a new plan to help you with maths. Should we ask your teacher for extra help? Would you like me to talk to her or do you want to do it?”
text: **{F1_FATHER}**: “Dit is frustrerend, my kind. Ek weet hoe hard jy geleer het. Miskien kan ons met 'n nuwe plan vorendag kom om jou met Wiskunde te help. Moet ons jou onderwyser vra vir ekstra hulp? Wil jy hê ek moet met haar praat, of wil jy dit self doen?”

SourceText: **{F1_DAUGHTER_16}**: “Thank you, {F1_FATHER}. I can do it! I think that might be a good idea.”
text: **{F1_DAUGHTER_16}**: “Dankie, Pa. Ek kan dit doen! Ek dink dis dalk 'n goeie idee.”

SourceText: **{F1_FATHER}**: “You know, maths may be difficult for you, but I am so proud of you for studying so hard, {F1_DAUGHTER_16}. Other people might give up, but you keep trying. That will help you so much in life!”
text: **{F1_FATHER}**: “Weet jy, Wiskunde is dalk vir jou moeilik, maar ek is so trots op jou dat jy so hard leer, {F1_DAUGHTER_16}. Ander mense sou dalk tou opgegooi het, maar jy hou aan probeer. Dit sal jou in die lewe so baie help!”

SourceText: 💛 Accept how you and your teen feel. Remember, your body and their body are sending messages about what you both are feeling, and those messages are important.
text: 💛 Aanvaar hoe jy en jou kind voel. Onthou, jou liggaam en hulle liggaam stuur boodskappe oor wat julle voel, en daardie boodskappe is belangrik.

SourceText: “I notice you slammed the door, talk loudly, and your face looks a bit upset. Are you feeling angry because you can’t go out at night?”
text: “Ek sien jy het die deur toegeklap, jy praat hard en jou gesig lyk 'n bietjie ontsteld. Voel jy kwaad omdat jy nie saans kan uitgaan nie?”

SourceText: 😃 Share your own feelings with your teen.
text: 😃 Deel jou eie gevoelens met jou tiener.

SourceText: 🎈This sets a good example and shows your teen that it's okay to share their own feelings.
text: 🎈Dit stel 'n goeie voorbeeld en wys vir jou tiener dat dit oukei is om hulle eie gevoelens te deel.

SourceText: ⭐ Do some active exercise to shake out anger, give each other some space, or take some deep breaths to calm down. These are some ways to deal with your emotions.
text: ⭐ Doen 'n paar aktiewe oefeninge om woede af te skud, gee vir mekaar ruimte of haal 'n paar keer diep asem om te kalmeer. Dit is 'n paar maniere om jou emosies te beheer.

SourceText: 🌷 Use actions and words to make your teen feel accepted and loved.
text: 🌷 Gebruik dade en woorde om jou tiener te help om aanvaar en geliefd te voel.

SourceText: 💚 Remind your teen that you are there for them and that they can always talk to you.
text: 💚 Herinner jou tiener dat jy daar is vir hulle en dat hulle altyd met jou kan praat.

SourceText: This lesson is about how we can keep calm and manage our anger when dealing with stressful moments with our teens. 💚
text: Hierdie les gaan oor hoe ons kalm kan bly en ons woede kan beheer wanneer ons stresvolle oomblikke met ons tieners beleef. 💚

SourceText: 🌷 We love our teens, but stress from caregiving and other events can make us feel angry. This is a normal part of life!
text: 🌷 Ons is lief vir ons tieners, maar die stres van versorging en ander gebeure kan ons kwaad laat voel! Dit is 'n normale deel van die lewe!

SourceText: 💡 It is important for us to stay in control and manage our anger so we do not hurt others.
text: 💡 Dit is vir ons belangrik om in beheer te bly en ons woede te beheer sodat ons nie ander seermaak nie.

SourceText: True or False: When I am angry at my teen, I should show that I am angry so that they stop misbehaving.
text: Waar of Vals: Wanneer ek kwaad is vir my tiener, moet ek wys dat ek kwaad is, sodat hulle kan ophou met hulle wangedrag.

SourceText: 💛 It can be very difficult when your teen is misbehaving, but you should try to manage your own stress and control your own anger before doing anything.
text: 💛 Dit kan baie moeilik wees wanneer jou tiener hulle wangedra, maar jy moet probeer om jou eie stres en woede te beheer voordat jy enigiets doen.

SourceText: {F2_GRANDMOTHER} is about to spend one-on-one time with {F2_SON_15} but finds the living room in a mess. She notices that this makes her feel angry.
text: {F2_GRANDMOTHER} gaan nou een-tot-een tyd saam met {F2_SON_15} deurbring, maar sien die woonkamer is baie deurmekaar. Sy let op dat dit haar kwaad laat voel.

SourceText: **{F2_GRANDMOTHER}** (thinking): “I’m feeling really angry. I am tired and my body feels tense. I just want to shout at {F2_SON_15}”
text: **{F2_GRANDMOTHER}** (dink): “Ek voel regtig kwaad. Ek is moeg en my liggaam voel gespanne. Ek wil net op {F2_SON_15} skree”

SourceText: **{F2_GRANDMOTHER}** (thinking): “I will Take a Pause and put on my favourite song.”
text: **{F2_GRANDMOTHER}** (dink): “Ek sal 'n bietjie wag en my gunsteling liedjie speel.”

SourceText: **{F2_GRANDMOTHER}** (thinking): “I am feeling a little better. I can think more clearly about what to do to support my teen.”
text: **{F2_GRANDMOTHER}** (dink): “Ek voel 'n klein bietjie beter. Ek kan nou helder dink oor wat om te doen om my tiener te ondersteun.”

SourceText: **{F2_GRANDMOTHER}**: “{F2_SON_15}, well done for doing your homework. You might  feel tired now. Let's take a 5-minute break, and after that, we can quickly pick up the mess together.”
text: **{F2_GRANDMOTHER}**: “{F2_SON_15}, dis mooi dat jy jou huiswerk gedoen het. Jy is dalk nou 'n bietjie moeg. Kom ons breek vir so 5 minute, dan kan ons gou saam die gemors opruim.”

SourceText: **{F2_GRANDMOTHER}**: “Thank you for doing your part to keep the house clean, {F2_SON_15}! I’m so proud of you for helping. Oh! It is our one-on-one time. What would you like to do?”
text: **{F2_GRANDMOTHER}**: “Dankie dat jy jou kant bring om die huis skoon te hou, {F2_SON_15}! Ek is so trots op jou dat jy help. O! Dis ons een-tot-een tyd. Wat wil jy graag doen?”

SourceText: ▶️Press Play to learn how to help you stay calm when your teen misbehaves. ☀️
text: ▶️Druk Speel om te leer hoe om jouself te help om kalm te bly wanneer jou tiener hulself wangedra. ☀️

SourceText: There are 4️⃣ steps to keeping calm in stressful situations: BE CALM, QUICK, CLEAR, and POSITIVE.
text: Daar is 4️⃣ stappe om in stresvolle situasies kalm te bly: WEES KALM, VINNIG, DUIDELIK en POSITIEF.

SourceText: Home Activity! Remember to use these four steps if you start feeling stressed or angry during one-on-one time with your teen. You can also share them with another member of your family. Sharing these lessons will be great for everyone’s well-being. ➡️
text: Tuisaktiwiteit! Onthou om hierdie vier stappe te gebruik as jy tydens een-tot-een tyd saam met jou tiener gestres of kwaad begin voel. Jy kan dit ook met 'n ander lid van jou gesin deel. Om hierdie wenke te deel, sal wonderlik wees vir almal se welstand. ➡️

SourceText: Amazing! ✨ Over the next few days, try to keep calm and redirect your teen from negative behaviour to a positive one before it gets worse.
text: Merkwaardig! ✨ Probeer oor die volgende paar dae om kalm te bly, en herlei jou tiener van negatiewe gedrag na positiewe gedrag voordat dit vererger.

SourceText: That's OK! Sometimes it takes time to begin using a new parenting lesson. 📝 Start by practising to Take a Pause even when you feel okay. This will help you be more ready when you feel stressed. ⭐
text: Dis oukei! Soms neem dit tyd om te begin om 'n nuwe ouerskaples te gebruik. 📝 Begin deur te oefen om 'n Blaaskans te neem, selfs wanneer jy oukei voel. Dit sal jou help om meer gereed te wees wanneer jy gestres voel. ⭐

SourceText: Well done! 🎉 You have learnt how to keep calm and redirect your teen when you are feeling stressed!
text: Welgedaan! 🎉 Jy het geleer hoe om kalm te bly en jou tiener te herlei wanneer jy gestres voel!

SourceText: ✅Take a pause or a few breaths before asking @fields.child_nickname to do something else.

SourceText: ✅ Remember your goal: Help @fields.child_nickname feel loved and connected to you.

SourceText: ✅Take a pause or a few breaths before asking your teen to do something else.
text: ✅Wag 'n bietjie of haal 'n paar keer asem voordat jy jou tiener vra om iets anders te doen.

SourceText: ✅ Speak with your teen in a calm voice.
text: ✅ Praat met jou tiener in 'n kalm stemtoon.

SourceText: ✅ Remember your goal: Help your teen feel loved and connected to you.
text: ✅ Onthou jou doelwit: Laat jou tiener geliefd voel en dat hulle 'n band met jou het.

SourceText: ✨ Praise something positive that your teen did today.
text: ✨ Prys iets positiefs wat jou tiener vandag gedoen het.

SourceText: ✨ Understand their feelings. For example, it may feel boring to clean up after having fun or ending a big task.
text: ✨ Verstaan hulle gevoelens. Dit kan byvoorbeeld vervelig voel om op te ruim nadat hulle pret gehad het of 'n groot taak afgehandel het.

SourceText: ✨ When you see @fields.child_nickname is about to start unwanted behaviour, distract them by asking them to do something else, before the unwanted behaviour begins or gets worse.
text: ✨ Wanneer jy sien @fields.child_nickname is op die punt om ongewenste gedrag te toon, lei hulle aandag af deur hulle te vra om iets anders te doen, voordat die ongewenste gedrag begin of erger word.
note: Strings like @fields.xxx and @results.yyy should not be translated.

SourceText: ✨ Invite @fields.child_nickname in a chore you are doing. You can ask @fields.child_nickname to think of a fun and quick way of doing the chores differently.
text: ✨ Nooi @fields.child_nickname om saam met jou 'n huistakie te doen. Jy kan @fields.child_nickname vra om aan 'n prettige en vinnige manier te dink om die taak anders te doen.
note: Strings like @fields.xxx and @results.yyy should not be translated.

SourceText: ✨ When you see your teen is about to start unwanted behaviour, distract them by asking them to do something else, before the unwanted behaviour begins or gets worse.
text: ✨ Wanneer jy sien jou tiener is op die punt om ongewenste gedrag te toon, lei hulle aandag af deur hulle te vra om iets anders te doen, voordat die ongewenste gedrag begin of erger word.

SourceText: ✨ Invite your teen in a chore you are doing. You can ask your teen to think of a fun and quick way of doing the chores differently.
text: ✨ Nooi jou tiener om saam met jou 'n huistakie te doen. Jy kan jou tiener vra om aan 'n prettige en vinnige manier te dink om die taak anders te doen.

SourceText: ✨ “Please speak more softly.” instead of “Stop shouting!”
text: ✨ “Praat asseblief sagter.” in plaas van “Hou op skree!”

SourceText: ✨ “Remember to hang your jacket in your cupboard, please.”
text: ✨ “Onthou asseblief om jou baadjie in jou kas te hang.”

SourceText: instead of “Don’t leave your jacket on the floor!”
text: in plaas van “Moenie jou baadjie op die vloer los nie!”

SourceText: ✅ Rather than saying what you DON’T want, tell your teen what you DO want them to do.
text: ✅ Eerder as om te sê wat jy NIE wil hê nie, sê vir jou tiener wat jy WiL hê hulle moet doen.

SourceText: ✅ Invite @fields.child_nickname to a positive activity when you see they are about to start an unwanted behaviour.
text: ✅ Nooi @fields.child_nickname na 'n positiewe aktiwiteit wanneer jy sien dat hulle ongewenste gedrag begin toon.
note: Strings like @fields.xxx and @results.yyy should not be translated.

SourceText: ✅ Praise your teen when they behave well!
text: ✅ Prys jou tiener vir goeie gedrag!

SourceText: ✅ Invite your teen to a positive activity when you see they are about to start an unwanted behaviour.
text: ✅ Nooi jou tiener vir 'n positiewe aktiwiteit wanneer jy sien dat hulle op die punt staan om ongewenste gedrag te begin toon.

SourceText: ⭐ Before you focus on supporting others, it’s important to take care of yourself.
text: ⭐ Voordat jy op die ondersteuning van ander fokus, is dit belangrik om eers na jouself te kyk.

SourceText: 😣 Feeling stressed as your children grow up is normal. As your relationship with them changes, it’s important to handle this shift well, and that starts with self-care.
text: 😣 Om gestres te voel terwyl jou kinders grootword is normaal. Soos jou verhouding met hulle verander, is dit belangrik om hierdie tydperk goed te hanteer, en dit begin met selfsorg.

SourceText: 🌱 Self-care means doing things for yourself to reduce stress and make you feel better.
text: 🌱 Selfsorg beteken om dinge vir jouself te doen om stres te verlig en jou beter te laat voel.

SourceText: ⏰ Let’s learn a simple way to handle everyday stress: Taking a pause.
text: ⏰ Kom ons leer 'n eenvoudige manier om alledaagse stres te hanteer: Vat 'n blaaskans.

SourceText: ♻ We will use this skill frequently in future lessons, so it is important to learn it today!
text: ♻ Ons sal hierdie vaardigheid gereeld in toekomstige lesse gebruik, so dit is belangrik om dit vandag te leer!

SourceText: Taking a pause will help you manage stress and be more able to take care of others and yourself.🌱
text: Om 'n blaaskans te neem, sal jou help om stres te beheer en jy sal meer in staat wees om jouself en ander te versorg.🌱

SourceText: ▶️Press Play to practise taking a pause together.
text: ▶️Druk Speel om te oefen om saam 'n blaaskans te neem.

SourceText: Try taking a pause whenever you feel angry, overwhelmed, stressed or worried. ⏰
text: Probeer om 'n blaaskans te neem wanneer jy ook al kwaad, oorweldig, gestres of bekommerd voel. ⏰

SourceText: Even a few deep breaths or connecting with the ground beneath you can make a difference. You can also take a pause with your child or teen.
text: Selfs 'n paar diep asemhalings, of om kontak te maak met die grond onder jou, kan 'n verskil maak. Jy kan ook saam met jou kind of tiener 'n blaaskans neem.

SourceText: Try taking a pause on a daily basis, even if you are feeling calm. Doing this regularly will help your brain take a pause, even in challenging situations.
text: Probeer om op 'n daaglikse basis 'n blaaskans te neem, selfs al voel jy kalm. As jy dit gereeld doen, sal dit jou brein help om 'n blaaskans te neem, selfs in uitdagende situasies.

SourceText: Well done! 🎉 You have learnt the importance of caring for yourself and how to take a pause. ✨
text: Welgedaan! 🎉 Jy het geleer van die belangrikheid om na jouself om te sien, en hoe om 'n blaaskans te neem. ✨

SourceText: 1️⃣ **SIT DOWN**
text: 1️⃣ **GAAN SIT**

SourceText: ⏸  Find a place where you can sit comfortably.
text: ⏸  Kry 'n plek waar jy gemaklik kan sit.

SourceText: ⭐ You can close your eyes if you want.
text: ⭐ Jy kan jou oë toemaak as jy wil.

SourceText: 2️⃣ **LISTEN**
text: 2️⃣ **LUISTER**

SourceText: 🌱 Take a deep breath.
text: 🌱 Haal diep asem.

SourceText: 👂 Feel the air moving in and out of your body.
text: 👂 Voel hoe die lug in en uit jou liggaam beweeg.

SourceText: 🔄 Repeat this 5 times.
text: 🔄 Herhaal dit 5 keer.

SourceText: 3️⃣ **NOTICE**
text: 3️⃣ **LET OP**

SourceText: ✨ Notice how your body feels while you breathe.
text: ✨ Let op hoe jou liggaam voel terwyl jy asemhaal.

SourceText: 🔵 Notice where you feel tension in your body and try to let it go.
text: 🔵 Let op waar jy spanning in jou liggaam voel, en probeer om dit te laat gaan.

SourceText: 4️⃣ **OPEN YOUR EYES**
text: 4️⃣ **MAAK JOU OË OOP **

SourceText: 👀 When you are ready, open your eyes.
text: 👀 Wanneer jy gereed is, maak jou oë oop.

SourceText: 🔑 Notice if you feel any different than when you started this activity.
text: 🔑 Let op of jy enigsins anders voel as toe jy met hierdie aktiwiteit begin het.
